steps.title: Spot the stark contrast in comfort ratings
steps.content:
The stem claims all three wanted cooler temps. But examine the comfort ratings: participant 20 reported −2 (feeling cold), while participants 1 and 21 both reported +1 (feeling warm).

That's a three-point gap on the −3 to +3 scale. The question signals a but — expecting us to explain why these different comfort levels coexist with the same temperature preference.

steps.title: Match the data to the answer
steps.content:
Choice C directly captures this contrast: participant 20 reported feeling significantly colder (−2) than the other two (+1 each).

This gap is not incidental; it explains why all three want cooler temps despite very different starting comfort levels.

Choices
  • A denies the difference
  • B references info not in the table
  • D reverses the relationship
.

That matches choice C — C is correct.
